steps.title: Set up the proportion
steps.content:
The ratio x to y is equivalent to the ratio 14 to t, so:
$$\frac{x}{y} = \frac{14}{t}$$
Substitute x = 210:
$$\frac{210}{y} = \frac{14}{t}$$

steps.title: Cross-multiply and isolate y
steps.content:
Cross-multiplying gives 210t = 14y.
Divide both sides by 14: y = 210t / 14 = 15t.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$15t$’: 15t — 210 / (15t) = 14/t ✓. That confirms A is correct.
  • Choice B — ‘$14t$’: 14t — 210 / (14t) = 15/t ≠ 14/t. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — ‘$196t$’: 196t — 210 / (196t) ≠ 14/t. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — ‘$224t$’: 224t — 210 / (224t) ≠ 14/t. Eliminated.
That confirms A is correct.
